title: Strong-to-Weak Spontaneous Symmetry Breaking
abstract: Strong-to-weak spontaneous symmetry breaking (SW-SSB) has recently emerged as a useful framework for studying phases of matter in open systems, quantum or classical. Beginning with the simple idea of extending symmetry breaking to general mixed states, and the familiar equivalence between canonical and grand-canonical ensembles in statistical mechanics, the concept has grown into a unifying perspective connecting many different ideas in physics, including topological orders, emergent hydrodynamics, and information-theoretic characterization of phases of matter. This review provides a bird's-eye view of some of these recent developments.
summary: This review introduces Strong-to-Weak Spontaneous Symmetry Breaking (SW-SSB) as a unifying concept for analyzing phases in open quantum systems. It shows that SW-SSB connects seemingly disparate fields, including topological order and emergent hydrodynamics, by using advanced tools like fidelity correlators and CMI. The work is crucial for understanding symmetry breaking in complex, realistic, and dissipative quantum matter.
main_problem: The paper develops Strong-to-Weak Spontaneous Symmetry Breaking (SW-SSB) as a unifying framework to study phases of matter in open quantum or classical systems.
main_result: SW-SSB provides a comprehensive connection between topological orders, emergent hydrodynamics, and information-theoretic characterizations of mixed-state phases.
method: The analysis employs advanced concepts from quantum information theory, comparing standard correlation functions with fidelity correlators and conditional mutual information.
model_or_system: The framework applies to general open quantum systems (described by density matrices $\rho$) and classical stochastic systems, using examples like the Ising ferromagnet in mixed states.
key_observables: Fidelity correlator ($\langle O angle_F$), Conditional Mutual Information (CMI), and the fidelity susceptibility ($\chi_ho^F$).
important_parameters: The distinction between strong symmetry (pure state) and weak symmetry (mixed state) breaking, and the role of the system size ($N$) or region size ($A$).
main_assumptions: The definition of SW-SSB is a conceptual framework connecting various physical ideas, and local measurements may not capture global properties.
figures_summary: Not specified (The notes summarize concepts rather than describing specific figures).
paper_structure: The review progresses by defining the core concept (SW-SSB), detailing its origins in statistical mechanics and information theory, presenting diagnostic tools (fidelity correlators, CMI), and finally unifying its application across topological order, hydrodynamics, and experimental platforms.
why_interesting: It provides a powerful, modern lens for understanding symmetry breaking in non-equilibrium and open quantum systems, linking concepts from statistical mechanics to quantum information theory.
analysis_basis: full PDF text, analyzed in chunks
